summary: Serum ferritin is the most sensitive test for detecting iron depletion during pregnancy because it reflects stored body iron, providing earlier indication of deficiency before clinically significant symptoms appear compared with measures assessing immediate iron status such as serum iron or total iron-binding capacity. It offers superior sensitivity by identifying depleted iron stores even when hemoglobin remains normal.
